Stage 1 vs Stage 2 component differences.

146567.title: Valkare's Tuning Compendium, Terminology, and Information
146567.author: valkare

credibility_breakdown.note: OP valkare not in contributors.json (C=2). Engine Management +1. E=0 (47K views < 50K threshold).

146567.general_knowledge_value: Comprehensive guide to ECU closed loop vs open loop operation, LTFT/STFT fuel trim theory, and how to use a piggyback tuner correctly. N/A target: 13.5 AFR at WOT. Closed loop stays at 14.7 AFR. Tune open loop first; closed loop tune is for LTFT preservation after battery reset or injector size changes. MAF signal manipulation is how piggybacks add/remove fuel.

100813.title: The Authoritative A/F Info Thread
100813.author: Diode Dynamics

credibility_breakdown.note: OP Diode Dynamics not in contributors.json (C=2). Engine Management +1. E=1 (66r, 53K views, 33 participants). CRITICAL: 2003+ Tiburons use 0v-5v wideband titanium sensors in stock ECU — narrowband A/F gauges do NOT work.

100813.general_knowledge_value: 2003+ Tiburons have 0v-5v wideband titanium O2 sensors in stock — narrowband gauges useless. Pre-03 used narrowband (gauges work differently). Wideband controller system needed: sensor + controller + wideband gauge, $150-600. Options: Innovate LC-1, AEM UEGO, Zeitronix Zt-2 (most popular). Key specs: 0.1 AFR accuracy, datalogging capability. Must weld O2 bung for sensor on post-03 cars.

89570.title: ecu pinout
89570.author: Raven

credibility_breakdown.note: OP Raven C=2, but Charlie-III (w=5) replied at post #19 — using max contributor weight C=5. Post_type corrected to question_post (P=-1). Engine Management +1. E=0 (6.9K views, 19 replies, 9 participants). IMPORTANT: pinout is for I4 2.0L only, NOT V6. Pinout images broken (2026).

89570.general_knowledge_value: I4 2.0L ECU pinout thread — pinout images broken (hosted on defunct host). Correction notes from Tuscanirider: pin 18 duplicate (first = correct wheel sensor), pin 10 = knock signal (NEO-confirmed), pin 66 = RPM signal (NEO-confirmed). V6 ECU (C133) is a different connector — this thread does NOT apply to V6 builds.

198171.title: I want headers but what about the CEL?
198171.author: Will51

credibility_breakdown.note: OP Will51 not in contributors.json (C=2). E=1 (188r, 1.3M views — highest view count in index by far). Participants estimated >10.

198171.general_knowledge_value: Headers remove pre-cats (built into stock manifolds), causing P0420/P0430 CEL. 4 O2 sensors total: 2 upstream primary (fuel control, never modify), 2 downstream secondary (cat efficiency only). 03-06: O2 spacers sometimes work, may avoid CEL. 07-08: WILL get CEL with headers, no reliable fix — need high-end cat + relocate O2 sensors and still not guaranteed. ECU reset won't help — OBD2 inspections check for incomplete ECU cycles. Bank 1 = firewall side (rear), Bank 2 = front of car.

215712.title: The NEW V6 Definitive Exhaust Thread
215712.author: caconman424

credibility_breakdown.note: OP caconman424 not in contributors.json (C=2). E=1 (150r, 67K views; participants estimated >10).

discontinued_parts: SFR single catback ($900, 3", QBuild=10 — no longer in production)
215712.general_knowledge_value: Crowd-sourced V6 exhaust comparison (scale 1-10: 1=loudest, 10=quietest). Borla: $600-700, 2.25", QBuild=9, QSound=10, Loudness=8 (best overall but small pipe). Aeon: $250, 2.25-2.5", QBuild=3, QSound=9, Loudness=6 (cheap but poor fitment). Greddy Evo 2: $900, 2.5", QBuild=8, QSound=4 (raspy), Loudness=3 (very loud). SFR single: $900 (discontinued), 3", QBuild=10, QSound=6, Loudness=7 (best for FI). ARK DT-S V2: ~$800-1379, 2.5" pipe/4.5" tip, QBuild=10, QSound=10, Loudness=6. Additional brands in replies (pages 2-6).

123286.title: Engine Compatibility Info
123286.author: Ravencrow

credibility_breakdown.note: OP Ravencrow not in contributors.json (C=2). E=1 (117r, 136K views; participants estimated >10). High value for G6BA swap/donor compatibility.

123286.general_knowledge_value: G6BA (2.7 Delta) donor vehicles: Sonata 2002-2005, Santa Fe 2001+, Tucson 2005+, Kia Optima 2001+ (2006.5+ is 2.7 Mu variant, +15hp), Kia Sportage 2005+. 3.5L Sigma mostly drop-in swap (common in Korea). 3.3L Lambda suitable but not drop-in.

127614.title: Official N/A Cams Information Thread
127614.author: faus03

credibility_breakdown.note: OP faus03 not in contributors.json (C=2). E=1 confirmed (117r, 71K views, 59 participants). chase206 is top contributor this month with 44 replies — has_priority_contributor updated to true.

127614.general_knowledge_value: Most common cam spec: .340 lift, .210 duration, .110 LSA (Crower E-64012). Crane: .370 lift, .216°, 116° LSA. Colt Tri-Flow for 2.7 delta: 218/210/218° duration, .365/.355/.365" lift. NGM Stage 1 ~$1000. Crower regrind process: $125-200 for donor cams + $400 regrind. Webcamshafts >$1000, minimum .350 lift for delta.

credibility_breakdown.note: OP Dingleweed not in contributors.json (C=2). Thread ID 100 — among the oldest threads on the forum. E=1 (84r, 131K views). Contains community dyno baselines and mod comparison pulls.

87013.title: How to build a custom TURBO
87013.author: tex2678

credibility_breakdown.note: OP tex2678 not in contributors.json (C=2). E=1 (83r, 135K views; participants estimated >10).

87013.general_knowledge_value: Custom turbo build parts list for V6 Tiburon. Mandatory: fuel tuner, 290cc+ injectors, 1:1 FPR, solid motor mounts, turbocharger, wastegate (38/44mm), BOV, intercooler, custom manifold, oil lines (-4an feed, -10an return), sandwich plate, heat protection, colder plugs, clutch/flywheel. Highly suggested: 255lph fuel pump, wideband, exhaust 2.5"/3". CRITICAL: 05+ requires returnless fuel system conversion. Remote mount turbo needs oil scavenge pump. Only commercial kit = NGM.

credibility_breakdown.note: OP dzignr_tastz not in contributors.json (C=2). Thread title is a question (P=0 as discussion_post). E=1 (104r, 81K views). Score may upgrade to reference_compilation (P=+2 → 4.1) after scraping if OP contains a guide.
